flashcards.front: What is the PMF of a Poisson distribution?
flashcards.back: P(X = k) = (e^(-λ) · λ^k) / k!, where k = 0, 1, 2, ... and λ is the mean rate.

flashcards.front: What is the mean and variance of a Poisson distribution?
flashcards.back: Both the mean and variance equal λ. E[X] = λ, Var(X) = λ.

flashcards.front: What is the PDF of a Normal distribution?
flashcards.back: f(x) = (1 / (σ√(2π))) · e^(-(x-μ)² / (2σ²)), where μ is the mean and σ is the standard deviation.

flashcards.front: What is the mean and variance of a Normal distribution?
flashcards.back: E[X] = μ (the mean parameter), Var(X) = σ² (square of standard deviation).

flashcards.front: How is the Poisson mean derived?
flashcards.back: Start with E[X] = Σk·P(X=k), substitute PMF, factor out λ·e^(-λ), use substitution j=k-1, recognize Taylor series for e^λ, cancel to get E[X] = λ.

flashcards.front: When do you use Poisson vs Normal distribution?
flashcards.back: Poisson: counting rare independent events in fixed intervals (discrete). Normal: modeling continuous measurements, natural phenomena, errors (continuous, symmetric bell curve).
